name: @browzer/skills
description: AI agent skills to empower your workflow with Browzer — hybrid vector + Graph RAG for codebases and document workspaces. Twenty-four skills around the `browzer` CLI: RAG search, dependency graph, doc ingestion, workspace management, and an end-to-end dev pipeline (brainstorming → generate-prd → generate-task → execute-task | execute-with-teams → code-review → receiving-code-review → write-tests → update-docs → feature-acceptance → commit → sync-workspace). Code-review spawns 4 mandatory parallel agents (senior-engineer, software-architect, qa, regression-tester); the regression-tester lane is non-collapsible, runs the canonical pre-push gate (`pnpm turbo test --filter='...[origin/main]'`) at PACKAGE granularity (not test-file), enumerates failures per-test, and reproduces each against `main` via git stash before marking `preExistingOnMain`. Receiving-code-review closes EVERY finding with a 7-step ladder for zero-tech-debt by default. Write-tests authors green coverage AND runs Stryker/mutmut/go-mutesting in the same pass; Phase 1.0 pre-flight probes test infra before declaring 'no setup'. Feature-acceptance Phase 1.5 live-verifies before deferring manual-AC items, with a §2.5.1 metric anti-soft-override regex that blocks autonomous flips of dashboard/browser/`/ask`/`/sync` metrics to `resolved:true` without operator reply. Orchestrate-task-delivery probes `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS` and prompts execution strategy (serial / parallel-worktrees / agent-teams); a `Stop` hook (orchestrator-autochain.py) enforces autonomous-mode chain continuation machine-checkably. Universal subagent preamble now requires `browzer deps --reverse` blast-radius probing before edits.
